segments.text: And Samuel died; and all the Israelites were gathered together, and lamented him, and buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose, and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
And there was a man in Maon, whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats: and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

Now the name of the man was Nabal; and the name of his wife Abigail: and she was a woman of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance: but the man was churlish and evil in his doings; and he was of the house of Caleb.
And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal did shear his sheep.
And David sent out ten young men,
Get you up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name:
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: greet…: Heb. ask him in my name of peace

segments.text: Peace be both to thee, and peace be to thine house, and peace be unto all that thou hast.
segments.speaker: David's Messengers

segments.text: Now I have heard that thou hast shearers: now thy shepherds which were with us, we hurt them not, neither was there ought missing unto them, all the while they were in Carmel.
segments.speaker: David's Messengers

segments.text: Ask thy young men, and they will shew thee. Wherefore let the young men find favour in thine eyes: for we come in a good day: give, I pray thee, whatsoever cometh to thine hand unto thy servants, and to thy son David.
segments.speaker: David's Messengers

segments.text: Who is David? and who is the son of Jesse? there be many servants now a days that break away every man from his master.
segments.speaker: Nabal

segments.text: Shall I then take my bread, and my water, and my flesh that I have killed for my shearers, and give it unto men, whom I know not whence they be?
segments.speaker: Nabal

segments.text: And David said unto his men,
Gird ye on every man his sword.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And they girded on every man his sword; and David also girded on his sword: and there went up after David about four hundred men; and two hundred abode by the stuff.
Upon me, my lord, upon me let this iniquity be: and let thine handmaid, I pray thee, speak in thine audience, and hear the words of thine handmaid.
segments.speaker: Abigail

segments.text: Blessed be the LORD God of Israel, which sent thee this day to meet me:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Blessed be the LORD, that hath pleaded the cause of my reproach from the hand of Nabal, and hath kept his servant from evil: for the LORD hath returned the wickedness of Nabal upon his own head.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And David sent and communed with Abigail, to take her to him to wife.
